article.body_preview:
Dhaka [Bangladesh], May 16 (ANI): Certain financial institutions in Bangladesh are exploring for Chinese payment channels to settle transactions with Russian banks that were removed from the SWIFT system to overcome the challenges experienced by importers and exporters as a result of Western sanctions following Russia's attack on Ukraine, The Asian Age reported.

The Bangladeshi government is apparently under pressure from some pro-Chinese high-ups to switch to the Chinese currency Yuan for fore

llm_connections.evidence_quote: Taka, the Bangladeshi currency, has lost nearly 25 per cent of its value against the dollar since Russia invaded Ukraine earlier this year
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly reports a near 25% decline in the national currency (BDT) versus the dollar, which is a country-level exchange-rate movement that falls within 'currency_log_return''s scope.

verification.quote_best_match: Taka, the Bangladeshi currency, has lost nearly 25 per cent of its value against the dollar since Russia invaded Ukraine earlier this year,
verification.judge_reasoning: The article contains the sentence stating the Taka "has lost nearly 25 per cent of its value against the dollar since Russia invaded Ukraine earlier this year, rocking global energy and commodities markets," which explicitly links the depreciation to the invasion's impact on markets and refers to the national currency (country-level).
